behavior_summary: SKULZ::look handles the 'U8P_SKULZ::look' (examine) event for a skull item. When invoked it marks the item with an info flag, modifies the process handling for that item, and makes the item 'bark' the label text "skull " to the player.

The handler uses a usecode 'set info' primitive (constant 0x0208) against the target process, then calls a 'process exclude' primitive on the same target, then calls Item::bark with the literal string "skull ".

The return value from Item::bark is combined with an implication/opcode and then the script suspends/returns. Overall effect: the skull is annotated as examined/changed and presents its short description to the player.
notable_control_flow: Ordered operations: set info (0x0208) on target -> process exclude on target -> Item::bark(target, "skull ") -> use bark return value with 'implies' -> suspend/return.
No global flags are written by this script; effects are local to the item/process and its visible output to the player.
The function executes as a process-level handler (it acts on a passed process/pid rather than as a pure pure function).
open_questions: Exact game semantics of the info flag 0x0208: it appears to mark the item as examined or set descriptive metadata, but its precise meaning is not certain.
Precise effect of 'process exclude' on the target process: likely prevents further automatic processing or re-triggering but the exact engine behavior is inferred.

How Item::bark's return value and the subsequent 'implies' opcode affect game flow: it appears to influence whether the U8P_SKULZ::look action is considered handled or whether further logic should run.
Whether any memory/temporary string cleanup beyond the observed free is required or occurs elsewhere (string lifetime is inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_SKULZ::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_SKULZ::look' (examine) action on a skull item: annotate the item/process with an info flag, exclude/modify its processing, and display the short label "skull " to the player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — triggered when the avatar (or actor) looks at / examines the skull item (area/interaction U8P_SKULZ::look event).

function_map.name: U8P_SKULZ::look
function_map.role: Handle the U8P_SKULZ::look/examine event for the skull item; orchestrates info-setting, process exclusion, and text output.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine that emits/displays a short text string for an item/process (used to present 'skull ').

function_map.name: U8P_SKULZ::set info (usecode primitive)
function_map.role: Engine primitive to set metadata/info flags on a process/item (called with 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_SKULZ::process exclude (usecode primitive)
function_map.role: Engine primitive that alters processing for a process (likely excludes it from further automatic processing or re-triggers).

external_calls.purpose: Apply info flag 0x0208 to the target process/item — inferred to mark the skull as examined or to set descriptive metadata.
Modify the target process so it is excluded from some kinds of subsequent processing (likely to prevent re-triggering or to change behavior after being examined).
Have the skull item emit/display the short label text "skull " to the player/process that looked at it.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: SKULZ::look handles the 'U8P_SKULZ::look' (examine) event for a skull item. When invoked it marks the item with an info flag, modifies the process handling for that item, and makes the item 'bark' the label text "skull " to the player.
